---

# Original Description
I was running on 0.12.3 **testnet** and decided to update to 0.13.0.2 and got this error.

```
2018-10-16 17:30:27,708 INFO  [default] Page size: 4096
2018-10-16 17:30:28.710	    7f7e81669780	INFO 	global	src/daemon/main.cpp:287	Monero 'Beryllium Bullet' (v0.13.0.2-release)
2018-10-16 17:30:28.710	    7f7e81669780	INFO 	global	src/daemon/protocol.h:53	Initializing cryptonote protocol...
2018-10-16 17:30:28.710	    7f7e81669780	INFO 	global	src/daemon/protocol.h:58	Cryptonote protocol initialized OK
2018-10-16 17:30:28.710	    7f7e81669780	INFO 	global	src/daemon/p2p.h:63	Initializing p2p server...
2018-10-16 17:30:32.715	    7f7e81669780	INFO 	global	src/daemon/p2p.h:68	p2p server initialized OK
2018-10-16 17:30:32.715	    7f7e81669780	INFO 	global	src/daemon/rpc.h:63	Initializing core RPC server...
2018-10-16 17:30:32.716	    7f7e81669780	INFO 	global	contrib/epee/include/net/http_server_impl_base.h:76	Binding on 127.0.0.1:28081
2018-10-16 17:30:32.716	    7f7e81669780	INFO 	global	src/daemon/rpc.h:69	core RPC server initialized OK on port: 28081
2018-10-16 17:30:32.716	    7f7e81669780	INFO 	global	src/daemon/core.h:86	Initializing core...
2018-10-16 17:30:32.716	    7f7e81669780	INFO 	global	src/cryptonote_core/cryptonote_core.cpp:447	Loading blockchain from folder /home/user/.bitmonero/testnet/lmdb ...
2018-10-16 17:30:32.716	    7f7e81669780	INFO 	global	src/blockchain_db/lmdb/db_lmdb.cpp:4071	Migrating blockchain from DB version 1 to 2 - this may take a while:
2018-10-16 17:31:01.851	    7f7e81669780	ERROR	cn	src/cryptonote_basic/cryptonote_format_utils.cpp:185	Failed to parse transaction from blob
2018-10-16 17:31:01.851	    7f7e81669780	WARN 	blockchain.db.lmdb	src/blockchain_db/lmdb/db_lmdb.cpp:75	Failed to parse tx from blob retrieved from the db
2018-10-16 17:31:01.853	    7f7e81669780	ERROR	cn	src/cryptonote_core/cryptonote_core.cpp:548	Error opening database: Failed to parse tx from blob retrieved from the db
2018-10-16 17:31:06.129	    7f7e81669780	INFO 	global	src/daemon/rpc.h:96	Deinitializing core RPC server...
2018-10-16 17:31:06.129	    7f7e81669780	INFO 	global	src/daemon/p2p.h:90	Deinitializing p2p...
2018-10-16 17:31:10.134	    7f7e81669780	INFO 	global	src/daemon/core.h:103	Deinitializing core...
2018-10-16 17:31:10.139	    7f7e81669780	ERROR	daemon	src/daemon/core.h:108	Failed to deinitialize core...
2018-10-16 17:31:10.139	    7f7e81669780	INFO 	global	src/daemon/protocol.h:75	Stopping cryptonote protocol...
2018-10-16 17:31:10.139	    7f7e81669780	INFO 	global	src/daemon/protocol.h:79	Cryptonote protocol stopped successfully
```

# Discussion History
## moneromooo-monero | 2018-10-16T17:59:41+00:00
That may be a corrupt db.
Can you log the offending tx, eg:
MGINFO("tx blob: " << bd);

Assuming the problem is where I think it is.
Then try running monerod again.

## aaronovz1 | 2018-10-16T18:51:56+00:00
Where exactly should I put that print statement? I assumed `parse_and_validate_tx_from_blob` but there is no `bd` variable and if I try print `tx_blob` it prints binary data to the console, so not really meaningful.

## moneromooo-monero | 2018-10-16T19:30:56+00:00
Try this:

<pre>
diff --git a/src/blockchain_db/lmdb/db_lmdb.cpp b/src/blockchain_db/lmdb/db_lmdb.cpp
index 288a0e4..d3c0805 100644
--- a/src/blockchain_db/lmdb/db_lmdb.cpp
+++ b/src/blockchain_db/lmdb/db_lmdb.cpp
@@ -4489,7 +4489,11 @@ void BlockchainLMDB::migrate_1_2()
       bd.assign(reinterpret_cast<char*>(v.mv_data), v.mv_size);
       transaction tx;
       if (!parse_and_validate_tx_from_blob(bd, tx))
+      {
+        MGINFO("Failed to parse tx:");
+        MGINFO("blob: " << epee::string_tools::buff_to_hex_nodelimer(bd));
         throw0(DB_ERROR("Failed to parse tx from blob retrieved from the db"));
+      }
       std::stringstream ss;
       binary_archive<true> ba(ss);
       bool r = tx.serialize_base(ba);
</pre>

## aaronovz1 | 2018-10-16T19:34:27+00:00
That prints a huge amount of text:
```
2018-10-16 19:33:26.476	    7f27d2502bc0	INFO 	global	src/blockchain_db/lmdb/db_lmdb.cpp:4071	Migrating blockchain from DB version 1 to 2 - this may take a while:
2018-10-16 19:33:26.489	    7f27d2502bc0	ERROR	cn	src/cryptonote_basic/cryptonote_format_utils.cpp:185	Failed to parse transaction from blob
2018-10-16 19:33:26.489	    7f27d2502bc0	INFO 	global	src/blockchain_db/lmdb/db_lmdb.cpp:4149	Failed to parse tx:
2018-10-16 19:33:26.489	    7f27d2502bc0	INFO 	global	src/blockchain_db/lmdb/db_lmdb.cpp:4150	blob: 
```

## moneromooo-monero | 2018-10-16T19:59:32+00:00
This is testnet, and you have a rct tx with type 4 signatures, which does not exist, but did for a bit before the format changed and testnet was reorg'd. Was your testnet database up and running and in sync before you updated from 0.12.3.0 ?

## aaronovz1 | 2018-10-16T21:13:32+00:00
Yep. I ran monerod and let it sync then I stopped it and swapped out the binaries for 0.13.0.2. It was showing a `X / Y` counter after `Migrating blockchain from DB version 1 to 2 - this may take a while:` which was increasing until it hit that error.

## iDunk5400 | 2018-10-16T21:18:12+00:00
What is the exact version of your v0.12.3.0 binaries you used to sync testnet ? `monerod --version`

## aaronovz1 | 2018-10-16T21:22:59+00:00
Monero 'Lithium Luna' (v0.12.3.0-release)

## moneromooo-monero | 2018-10-16T21:26:27+00:00
Then you were most probably on an old testnet fork, with incompatible proof format (that type 4 thing), and the conversion process is not recognizing it. You will have to either:
- resync testnet from scratch
- actually, you can't do the other thing anymore because the database is now half converted


## aaronovz1 | 2018-10-16T21:29:29+00:00
I guess I'll delete entire DB and resync the whole thing again. For reference though, this is the second time this has happened to me. I believe going from 0.11 to 0.12 I encountered a similar thing. What is the right way to upgrade without getting this issue?

## iDunk5400 | 2018-10-16T21:35:19+00:00
Best is not to run a -release daemon on testnet.

## aaronovz1 | 2018-10-16T21:40:15+00:00
I'm a little confused on what should be run then. I grab releases from: https://github.com/monero-project/monero/releases - where are specific testnet releases located?

## iDunk5400 | 2018-10-16T21:42:19+00:00
There aren't any. Users of testnet build their own binaries from master branch. If you need to test something, please use stagenet instead, which is compatible with -release binaries.

## aaronovz1 | 2018-10-16T21:54:59+00:00
Thanks for clearing this up. I'll use stagenet from now on.

# Action History
- Created by: aaronovz1 | 2018-10-16T17:45:25+00:00
- Closed at: 2018-10-16T21:54:59+00:00
